food_subgroup: Savoury pastry snack — deep-fried or baked curry puff with spiced potato and chicken filling

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03302114 represents the generic curry puff category. The Curry'O is a proprietary Old Chang Kee variant; no exact FoodOn code exists for this branded product.
ontology.foodex2_description: Pastry, filled, savoury — used as closest parent for curry puff / karipap
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists specifically for Singapore curry puff. A0CKN (filled savoury pastry) is the nearest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for curry puff or karipap as a distinct food entity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Curry'O curry puff from Old Chang Kee weighs approximately 65 g and is typically consumed as a single-piece snack.

nutrient_source.title: Curry Puff (Karipap) — HPB Singapore Foodo / Health Promotion Board Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo lists curry puff (karipap) with a spiced potato-chicken filling and deep-fried wheat pastry shell, which is the closest approved primary source match for Old Chang Kee Curry'O. Values are adapted from HPB Foodo curry puff entry per 100 g edible portion. Old Chang Kee Curry'O is a proprietary variant; no exact branded nutrient data is available in an approved source. HPB Foodo is the highest-priority approved source for Singapore dishes.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Savoury Pastry / Filled Wheat Pastry — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate based on wheat-flour pastry with potato filling
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Old Chang Kee Curry'O or Singapore curry puff in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 55 is estimated from the closest analogous food: wheat-flour savoury pastry with potato filling, which has a reported GI in the low-to-medium range (approximately 50–60) in the University of Sydney GI Database and supporting peer-reviewed literature. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 19.8) / 100 ≈ 10.8, where carbohydrate per 65 g serving = 30.5 × 0.65 = 19.8 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~380 mg per 100 g; ~247 mg per piece)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit curry puff consumption to one piece occasionally, as the sodium content from seasoned filling and pastry contributes meaningfully to daily sodium targets of under 2000 mg; pairing with low-sodium foods throughout the day is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat pastry and starchy potato filling yield a low glycemic index (~55) and moderate glycemic load (~10.8 per piece)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume curry puff in moderation — ideally one piece as part of a mixed meal — to blunt the postprandial glucose response from the refined wheat pastry and potato filling, which has a low glycemic index (~55) and moderate glycemic load (~10.8 per piece); portion control and pairing with non-starchy vegetables or protein is recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~6.8 g per 100 g) and sodium from deep-frying and seasoned filling
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit curry puff intake due to its saturated fat content from deep-frying and the moderate sodium load; occasional consumption of one piece is acceptable within a heart-healthy dietary pattern that is otherwise low in saturated fat and sodium.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Curry'O is a proprietary curry puff sold by Old Chang Kee, a well-known Singaporean fast-food chain specialising in deep-fried snacks. It is a savoury pastry snack consisting of a flaky, deep-fried wheat flour shell encasing a spiced filling of diced potato, chicken, and onion seasoned with curry powder and aromatics. The pastry is characteristically golden-brown, crispy on the outside, and slightly chewy within, while the filling is moist, warmly spiced, and mildly peppery. Curry puff (karipap in Malay) is one of Singapore's most iconic street snacks, with roots in British Cornish pasty and Indian samosa traditions adapted to local Malay and Chinese tastes during the colonial era. Old Chang Kee has been selling curry puffs since 1956 and the Curry'O is their flagship product, priced at approximately SGD 2.20 per piece. Nutritionally, one piece (approximately 65 g) provides around 189 kcal, 4.2 g protein, 10.4 g fat, and 19.8 g carbohydrate. The low glycemic index (~55) and moderate glycemic load (~10.8) make it a food that people with Type 2 Diabetes should consume in moderation. The sodium content (~247 mg per piece) is relevant for individuals managing hypertension. The saturated fat from deep-frying is a consideration for cardiovascular health. Curry'O is halal-certified and widely available across Singapore at Old Chang Kee outlets in hawker centres, MRT stations, and shopping malls.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a curry puff?
Is Old Chang Kee curry puff healthy?

What is the GI of curry puff?
Old Chang Kee Curry'O nutrition facts

confidence_reason: HPB Foodo provides the closest approved primary source for Singapore curry puff (karipap) with a spiced potato-chicken filling and deep-fried wheat pastry shell. Old Chang Kee Curry'O is a proprietary branded product; no exact branded nutrient data is available in any approved source. Values are adapted from the HPB Foodo curry puff entry, which is the highest-priority approved source for Singaporean dishes. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on wheat-flour savoury pastry with potato filling. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Foodo Singapore curry puff (karipap) entry per 100 g edible portion. Old Chang Kee Curry'O is a proprietary product; exact branded nutrient data is not available in any approved source. 2. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source for this food category. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source for this food category. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source for this food category. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source for this food category. 6. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on wheat-flour savoury pastry with potato filling from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for Singapore curry puff or Old Chang Kee Curry'O. 7. Halal status is based on Old Chang Kee's publicly stated halal certification from MUIS (Islamic Religious Council of Singapore). 8. Serving size of 65 g is based on the approximate weight of one standard Old Chang Kee curry puff piece as reported in publicly available nutritional references for this product category.
